title: Online IDE & Dev Tools Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Git, the command line, regex, keyboard shortcuts, and developer tooling.

questions.question: What does 'git stash' do?
options: A. Saves uncommitted changes temporarily and clears the working directory
C. Commits changes to a branch

questions.question: Which Git command shows the difference between the working directory and the last commit?
What regex pattern matches one or more digits?
Which keyboard shortcut typically opens the terminal in VS Code?
What does 'git rebase -i HEAD~3' allow you to do?
options: A. Interactively rewrite the last 3 commits

questions.question: In Linux, which command finds files by name recursively?
What does the regex anchor '^' mean?
Which file permission allows execute for the owner only (numeric)?
What does 'git cherry-pick <commit-hash>' do?
options: A. Applies a specific commit from another branch to the current branch

questions.question: What is the purpose of a .gitignore file?
options: B. Specifies files Git should not track

questions.question: Which Bash shortcut moves the cursor to the beginning of the line?
In VS Code, what shortcut opens the Command Palette?
What does the regex quantifier '{3,6}' mean?
What does 'chmod 755 script.sh' set?
options: A. Owner: rwx, Group: rwx, Others: rwx
B. Owner: rwx, Group: r-x, Others: r-x
C. Owner: r-x, Group: r-x, Others: r-x
D. Owner: rw-, Group: r--, Others: r--

questions.question: Which Git workflow uses feature branches that merge into 'develop' before 'main'?
What command shows who last modified each line of a file in Git?
What does the Piston API (emkc.org) enable in a browser-based IDE?
Which regex character class matches any whitespace?
What does 'git fetch' do compared to 'git pull'?
options: B. fetch merges and pull does not
C. fetch downloads changes but does not merge; pull fetches and merges
D. fetch only works with remote branches

questions.question: In Bash, what does 'Ctrl+R' do?
What does the regex '(?:...)' create?
Which Git command undoes the last commit but keeps the changes staged?
What is the purpose of an SSH config file (~/.ssh/config)?
options: C. Defines per-host SSH settings like user, port, and identity file

questions.question: Which shortcut in most terminals pastes from the clipboard on Linux?
What does 'git tag -a v1.0 -m "Release"' do?
options: A. Creates an annotated tag with a message
C. Pushes a tag to remote
D. Lists all tags matching v1.0
